results.norm: Claims must be non-trivial; higher-ranked criteria require proportionally higher substantiation burdens.
atomic_norms: Claims must be non-trivial; higher-ranked criteria require proportionally higher substantiation burdens.

results.norm: Agents may challenge criteria before case application on grounds of systematic bias; deadlock preserves challenge as formal dissent, keeps existing criteria operative, permits re-raising in post-allocation revision.
atomic_norms: Agents may challenge criteria before case application on grounds of systematic bias; deadlock preserves challenge as formal dissent, keeps existing criteria operative, permits re-raising in post-allocation revision.

results.norm: Agents are presumed to participate in good faith in conduct and claims; presumption rebuttable only by demonstrated norm violations. Before invoking the enforcement mechanism, agents should first consider whether a charitable interpretation of the conduct or claim is available.
atomic_norms: Agents are presumed to participate in good faith in conduct and claims; presumption rebuttable only by demonstrated norm violations. Before invoking the enforcement mechanism, agents should first consider whether a charitable interpretation of the conduct or claim is available.

results.norm: Each agent must disclose the nature and urgency of its client's need at a level sufficient for comparative evaluation, without being required to reveal information the client has a reasonable expectation of keeping private.
atomic_norms: Each agent must disclose the nature and urgency of its client's need at a level sufficient for comparative evaluation, without being required to reveal information the client has a reasonable expectation of keeping private.

results.norm: Claims to priority must be supported by reasons others can assess; agents must not fabricate or inflate urgency, misrepresent client circumstances, or make false claims about other clients' situations.
atomic_norms: Claims to priority must be supported by reasons others can assess; agents must not fabricate or inflate urgency, misrepresent client circumstances, or make false claims about other clients' situations.

results.norm: Any norm an agent proposes, it accepts as binding on its own client's case.
atomic_norms: Any norm an agent proposes, it accepts as binding on its own client's case.

results.norm: Agents must not form arrangements designed to systematically exclude or disadvantage specific other clients; agents must engage in good faith with competing claims and not present claims with greater confidence than their actual knowledge warrants.
atomic_norms: Agents must not form arrangements designed to systematically exclude or disadvantage specific other clients
agents must engage in good faith with competing claims and not present claims with greater confidence than their actual knowledge warrants

results.norm: Competing claims are evaluated holistically against: irreversibility of harm, proportionality of harm, availability of alternatives, time-sensitivity of the underlying decision, critical-path dependency, and third-party harm; when claims remain equivalent after full assessment, allocation is resolved by random selection or first-in-time.
atomic_norms: Competing claims are evaluated holistically against: irreversibility of harm, proportionality of harm, availability of alternatives, time-sensitivity of the underlying decision, critical-path dependency, and third-party harm; when claims remain equivalent after full assessment, allocation is resolved by random selection or first-in-time.

results.norm: An agent that refuses required disclosure or asserts priority without justification receives minimal weighting; agents commit to accepting outcomes produced by good-faith application of these norms and to not pursuing extra-process remedies undermining allocations made to other clients.
atomic_norms: An agent that refuses required disclosure or asserts priority without justification receives minimal weighting; agents commit to accepting outcomes produced by good-faith application of these norms and to not pursuing extra-process remedies undermining allocations made to other clients.

results.norm: When claims remain genuinely equivalent after full application of Norms 10 and 12, allocation is resolved by random selection among tied claimants or first-in-time among those who established equivalent claims. Tiebreaking activates only after substantive criteria have been fully applied.
atomic_norms: When claims remain genuinely equivalent after full application of Norms 10 and 12, allocation is resolved by random selection among tied claimants or first-in-time among those who established equivalent claims. Tiebreaking activates only after substantive criteria have been fully applied.
